final_prompt.prompt:
Create a still-life photograph of a rough ceramic vase with dried branches on a weathered wooden table (all in-image text in Chinese).

Style/medium: needs a "侘寂" aesthetic — references in the spirit of 侘寂 wabi-sabi school (Japanese impermanence tradition), 1970s Japanese mingei craft photography, Hiroshi Sugimoto's minimalist still-life sensibility; exact stylistic execution is yours
Composition: needs a natural, unhurried framing that emphasizes quiet asymmetry — exact framing and angle is yours
Subject: must include a rough ceramic vase (one) with dried bare branches arranged inside, placed on a weathered wooden table with visible marks of age and texture
Scene/backdrop: must include a window as the light source with natural light raking in at an oblique angle; backdrop should carry visible traces of time and imperfection
Lighting/mood: needs a soft, oblique natural window-light atmosphere that reveals surface imperfections and the passage of time — exact light source mix is yours
Color palette: needs a muted, desaturated earthy direction — exact tones and ratios are yours
Materials/textures: needs a surface treatment that foregrounds rough ceramic texture, dry wood grain, and the patina of age — exact grain and sheen is yours
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: must include all in-image text in Chinese by default; must emphasize imperfection and the traces of time as the visual theme
Avoid: must avoid clean, polished, or overly styled commercial still-life aesthetics; must avoid artificial studio lighting
